name: Architext 1.7.6 Native-Only Distribution + Flow Decision Diamonds
summary: Distribution becomes native-only: the entire npm channel is removed — the @robotaccomplice/architext launcher package, the per-platform binary packages (optionalDependencies), package.json/package-lock.json, the npm build/publish/CI steps, and the npm Just recipes. The product version moves off package.json to a repo-root VERSION file that build.rs stamps into the binary (no Node needed to build or test). The curl installer + GitHub-release binaries (with the viewer dist and schemas embedded) + the in-binary `architext update` self-updater are the sole supported install; `architext update` keeps the npm→native off-ramp for users still on the old bridge. Flow decision diamonds are completed per the settled design (viewer/DESIGN.md): a decision diamond now carries an anchoring stem to its host node, a per-branch outcome label, and a what-is-being-decided annotation, instead of rendering as an orphaned floating rect. The README screenshots and the Architext self-model are reconciled to the native-only reality.
updateSource: Shipped 2026-06-22: GitHub release v1.7.6 published 2026-06-22T18:51:33Z with all five native binaries + SHA256SUMS attached by the rewritten native-only publish.yml (validated by a dry-run first). Install-path verified end to end: the darwin-arm64 asset downloaded, its SHA256 matched, and the binary reports 1.7.6; the dry-run's smoke ran sync + validate on the self-contained binary from outside the repo. npm registry correctly stays at 1.7.5 (no 1.7.6 npm publish — native-only). Diamonds live-verified on the FlowForge corpus AND architext's own data; routing + viewer suites green plus the end-to-end stem-geometry test.

required.title: Flow decision diamonds: stem + branch outcome labels + annotation
required.summary: A decision step rendered as a floating, unconnected rect — missing all three elements the settled contract requires. Implement them: (1) an anchoring STEM, a routed connector from the diamond to its host node (built in plan_request as a synthetic relationship, host-bottom → diamond-top; a clean straight vertical because the diamond's center-x equals its host's, so it never trips the §0 dogleg/Z gate; rendered without an arrowhead as EdgeKind::Stem); (2) per-branch OUTCOME labels (step.outcome, e.g. valid/invalid), read straight from the flow and anchored just outside the diamond on each branch line — the data was already carried through BuiltRelationship.outcome and only discarded at render; (3) an ANNOTATION captioning the diamond with the decision step's action (what is being decided), placed above the diamond where no branch can collide. Selecting the decision step co-highlights the whole cluster. Live-verified on the corpus and architext's own data.

required.title: Remove the entire npm distribution channel (native-only)
required.summary: The final step of the npm phase-out: cut ALL npm. Delete the @robotaccomplice/architext launcher (tools/architext-adopt.mjs + native-binary-resolver.mjs), the per-platform binary packages (optionalDependencies) and their build script, package.json + package-lock.json, the npm packaging scripts (build-binary-packages, smoke-packed-cli, check-install-docs, smoke-native-package), and .npmignore. The product version moves to a repo-root VERSION file (build.rs stamps ARCHITEXT_VERSION from it — was reading package.json). publish.yml drops the npm stamp/publish/OIDC/npm-smoke job and keeps build + a new release job that attaches the five binaries + SHA256SUMS to the GitHub release; ci.yml drops its npm package job (the verify-rust job already builds with trunk and validates with cargo); release-binaries.yml's native-smoke runs the self-contained binary directly; the Justfile npm recipes are removed. The native path (install.sh + `architext update`) is the sole install; the in-binary npm→native migration off-ramp is kept since the npm package shipped through 1.7.5. The copied-install migration ramp stays until 2.0.0 (roadmap retire-copied-install-migration).

required.title: Reconcile docs, self-model, and README screenshots to native-only
required.summary: Bring the docs and the Architext self-model in line with native-only distribution: README drops the npm badge + 'Via npm' install section (Distribution now describes GitHub-release binaries + SHA256SUMS) and keeps an `architext update` off-ramp note; viewer/README + CONTRIBUTING replace the dead `npm run *` commands with the real cargo/trunk/just workflow; CLI messages/comments stop pointing at `npm run build` and the deleted launcher. The self-model is reconciled from source: the 'NPM package artifacts' node becomes 'Native release artifacts', the release-packaging flow's npm-pack step becomes the binary-attach step, and the npm-pack risk/decision/glossary references move to native — keeping the package-artifacts node id so references stay valid. All eight README screenshots are regenerated at 1680x1000 from architext's own served data, including the now-anchored decision diamond.

workstreams.name: Diagram fidelity
workstreams.summary: Complete the flow decision diamond per the settled design: stem, branch outcome labels, and annotation.

workstreams.name: npm phase-out (final)
workstreams.summary: Remove the entire npm channel, move the version source to VERSION, and reconcile docs + the self-model to native-only.

evidence.label: Decision diamonds live-verified via Playwright DOM on the FlowForge corpus AND architext's own data (vertical stem, no arrowhead; outcomes + annotation, zero collisions; cluster co-highlight; 0 console errors)

End-to-end stem-geometry test through the real build_flow_plan_request → plan_diagram path asserts a legal vertical stem clearing the §0 gate (crates/architext-routing/tests/decision_stem_geometry.rs); routing 370 + viewer 127 green
All three workflows (publish/ci/release-binaries) rewritten npm-free; actionlint clean
Version source moved package.json → VERSION; build.rs stamps it; `architext --version` reports 1.7.6 with no Node
Self-model reconciled to native-only and passes the native validator (cargo run -p architext-cli -- validate .)
